channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Sadiq Khan pledges increased policing and education to combat rising antisemitism in London
item.description: In this opinion piece, London Mayor Sadiq Khan addresses the growing fear within the Jewish community in London amid a surge in antisemitic incidents. He acknowledges that Jewish Londoners are experie...

item.title: How parallel political structures are reshaping West Bengal politics
item.description: The article examines how West Bengal’s politics has evolved into a system where the formal party machinery is no longer the only point of contact with voters. In a village in Jalpaiguri, a woman misto...

item.title: Benguet reopens trekking trails and eco-tourism sites after forest fires
item.description: ITOGON, Benguet — Trekking and other outdoor activities are now allowed again in Itogon, Benguet as the local government lifted the suspension of eco-tourism sites starting Friday. Mayor Bernard Wacli...

item.title: Election Commission Confirms Full Security and Smooth Counting in West Bengal
item.description: Ahead of the West Bengal Assembly elections vote counting on May 4, the Election Commission (EC) has reassured that all arrangements are complete to ensure a smooth and peaceful process. West Bengal C...

item.title: The unexplained deaths of UFO researchers over the years and possible connections
item.description: A recent investigation into the mysterious disappearances and deaths of UFO researchers has sparked a renewed interest in cases stretching back decades. Since 2022, at least 11 prominent scientists, i...

item.title: Saleswoman wins tribunal over unfair commission changes at pest control firm
item.description: Dawn Piper, a senior sales manager at Rentokil Initial Ltd, successfully claimed constructive unfair dismissal after the company introduced a new commission scheme that threatened the majority of her ...

item.title: Labor weighs its reform appetite as Albanese faces key budget and political test
item.description: The Guardian article examines the Albanese Labor government in its fourth year, highlighting an internal debate about its mission and willingness to pursue bold reform. After a record‑winning 2025 ele...

item.title: Pope Leo XIV Names New Bishop for Urdaneta Diocese
item.description: On May 3, 2026, Pope Leo XIV officially appointed a new bishop for the Diocese of Urdaneta. The appointment marks a significant moment for the local Catholic community, as the bishop will be tasked wi...

item.title: Kidnappers Demand N150 Million for 16 Abducted Worshippers in Ekiti
item.description: Kidnappers who had earlier demanded a N1 billion ransom for the release of 16 worshippers abducted from a Christ Apostolic Church (CAC) in Eda-Oniyo, Ekiti State, have reduced their demand to N150 mil...

item.title: Shakira Draws Millions to Copacabana Beach in Rio for Free Concert
item.description: Shakira turn Rio Copacabana Beach to wahala as she hold her mega free concert under full moon. Dey expect around two million people for the show, wey she perform on big stage near the famous Copacaban...

item.title: Jopay Paguia appears as second celebrity player on Kapamilya Deal or No Deal
item.description: ABS-CBN, one of the leading media and entertainment companies in the Philippines, continues to bring exciting content to its audience with popular programs. Recently, Sexbomb member Jopay Paguia made ...

item.title: Pretoria High Court freezes Wicknell Chivayo’s assets amid cross-border divorce dispute
item.description: The Gauteng Division of the High Court in Pretoria has reinstated an interim order freezing key assets linked to businessman Wicknell Chivayo as part of an ongoing cross-border divorce dispute with hi...

item.title: Premier League Confirms Sesko’s Goal vs Liverpool Stood After VAR Review
item.description: Benjamin Sesko scored Manchester United’s second goal in their 3-2 victory over Liverpool on May 3, but the strike raised questions as the ball seemed to brush his fingers. After careful VAR review, t...

item.title: Hiker Dies After Falling Into Ravine on Mt. Wilson Trail in Los Angeles County
item.description: A male hiker died on Saturday after falling a significant distance into a ravine along a trail on Mt. Wilson in Los Angeles County, according to local authorities. The Los Angeles Sheriff’s Department...

item.title: Joey Walsh impresses on debut as Penrith Panthers edge Manly Sea Eagles in tight clash
item.description: Penrith Panthers have edged out Manly Sea Eagles 18-16 in a tightly contested NRL clash, ending Manly’s four-game winning streak under coach Kieran Foran. Despite the loss, Foran described the perform...

item.title: Ayush Tiwari awarded 2025 ACJ prize for groundbreaking cybercrime investigation
item.description: Ayush Tiwari of Scroll.in has been conferred the 2025 Asian College of Journalism (ACJ) award in the category of investigative journalism. He was recognised for his extensive investigative series on c...

item.title: Two people killed in Bristol explosion linked to domestic dispute
item.description: Authorities in Bristol have confirmed the deaths of a woman and a man following an explosion at a residential property. The incident, which occurred in a domestic setting, is being treated as related ...

item.title: KZN police officers killed in targeted attacks, union and community condemn violence
item.description: Police officers in KwaZulu-Natal have been tragically targeted in two separate incidents this past week, prompting strong condemnation from both the KZN Community Policing Forum (CPF) and the Police a...

item.title: Minister Orders Investigation into Delayed Care at Muhimbili Hospital
item.description: On May 3, 2026, Tanzanian Minister for Health Mohamed Mchengerwa directed an immediate investigation into the delays in specialist care at Muhimbili National Hospital. Complaints had emerged from pati...

item.title: Calls Grow for More Resources and Stronger Enforcement in Newfoundland and Labrador Workplace Safety
item.description: The Newfoundland and Labrador government has not increased funding for its occupational health and safety (OH&S) division, despite repeated calls from the provincial federation of labour for stronger ...
